nodes.title: In Vitro Pharmacology Package
nodes.content: Completed summary node extracted from the paper. This package combines Schrödinger docking against PDE4A10, isoform-level cAMP inhibition assays across PDE4 family members, and anti-inflammatory testing in RAW264.7 macrophages, IMG microglia-like cells, and human PBMCs. Together these experiments established that 11h is a potent broad PDE4 inhibitor with low-nanomolar biochemical activity and a clear anti-inflammatory effect in murine and human immune-cell systems.

nodes.title: In Vivo Neuroinflammation Proof of Concept
nodes.content: Completed summary node extracted from the paper. This package covers the mouse LPS neuroinflammation study with brain immune-cell phenotyping and the paired RT-qPCR analysis of the MyD88 inflammatory signaling axis. The work showed that 11h reduced inflammatory markers, shifted macrophage phenotype, reduced CD4-positive cells, and downregulated pathway genes linked to neuroinflammation.

nodes.title: PK and Brain Penetration Package
nodes.content: Completed summary node extracted from the paper. This package groups the rat IV and oral PK studies, repeat-dose PK, tissue distribution work, and red blood cell partitioning assay. The paper reported oral exposure, repeat-dose accumulation, strong brain penetration with sustained brain concentrations above plasma, and limited red blood cell partitioning, supporting continued CNS development.

nodes.title: Exploratory Safety and Tolerability Package
nodes.content: Completed summary node extracted from the paper. This package combines CYP inhibition, CiPA ion channel profiling, Ames mutagenicity, broad off-target screening, mouse MTD, rat repeat-dose tolerability, and the ferret emesis and TNFalpha pharmacodynamic study. The package suggested low off-target and cardiac liability, no convincing genotoxic concern in the exploratory screen, wide tolerability margins, and lower emetic liability than expected for the PDE4 class.

nodes.title: Behavioral Safety and CNS Signal Package
nodes.content: Completed summary node extracted from the paper. This package covers the elevated plus maze, elevated zero maze, and forced swim test work. The paper used these assays to show that repeated dosing with 11h did not produce obvious adverse neurobehavioral effects in healthy rodents and also generated early anxiolytic-like and antidepressant-like signals.

nodes.title: Development Candidate Review and IND Gap Assessment
nodes.content: Future program gate. Consolidate the paper results into a formal development-candidate review and define the IND-enabling gaps that still need to be closed. The output should be a decision to advance 11h, an initial target product profile, and a list of the specific CMC, toxicology, bioanalytical, and regulatory workstreams needed to reach an IND filing.

nodes.title: CMC Readiness Package
nodes.content: Future summary node. This package combines scalable API process development, analytical method setup, impurity strategy, solid-state assessment, toxicology and clinical formulation selection, GMP API manufacture, drug product manufacture, and initial stability support. The package must deliver releaseable material and phase-appropriate CMC documentation suitable for both definitive toxicology studies and the IND.

nodes.title: DMPK Translation and Dose Projection
nodes.content: Future summary node. This package includes plasma protein binding, brain unbound fraction, hepatocyte stability, metabolite identification, reaction phenotyping, permeability and transporter studies, and integrated human exposure projection. The goal is to convert the exploratory PK findings from the paper into a robust translational dosing framework and to justify the definitive tox species and first-in-human starting dose assumptions.

nodes.title: GLP Genotox and Bioanalytical Readiness
nodes.content: Future summary node. This package groups the GLP Ames confirmation study, the in vitro micronucleus assay, and the validated bioanalytical methods needed to support toxicokinetic analysis in definitive safety studies. It converts exploratory safety screens from the paper into regulatory-grade assays tied to the final development material.

nodes.title: Core Safety Pharmacology Package
nodes.content: Future summary node. This package covers the in vivo cardiovascular telemetry study and focused CNS and respiratory safety pharmacology batteries. The purpose is to complement the exploratory in vitro cardiac and behavioral findings from the paper with a more formal set of integrated safety pharmacology studies expected before first-in-human dosing.

nodes.title: Rat and Dog Dose-Range Finding Toxicology
nodes.content: Future summary node. Run short repeat-dose non-GLP range-finding studies in rat and dog with toxicokinetic sampling to identify dose-limiting findings, target organs, and the dose levels to carry into definitive GLP studies. This package is the bridge between the exploratory tolerability signals in the paper and the actual IND-enabling repeat-dose tox design.

nodes.title: GLP Repeat-Dose Toxicology in Rat and Dog
nodes.content: Future summary node. Conduct definitive 28-day GLP repeat-dose oral toxicology studies in rat and dog, both with toxicokinetic sampling, clinical pathology, necropsy, organ weights, and histopathology. These studies establish the nonclinical safety foundation for the IND and provide the exposure margins and no-observed-adverse-effect levels needed to support human entry.

nodes.title: Pre-IND Strategy, Meeting, and Clinical Planning
nodes.content: Future summary node. Prepare the pre-IND briefing package, hold the FDA meeting, close action items, draft the investigator brochure, and prepare the first-in-human single-ascending-dose protocol and starting-dose rationale. This package converts the nonclinical and CMC work into a coherent regulatory and clinical entry strategy.

nodes.title: IND Authoring and Filing Readiness
nodes.content: Future final integration node. Assemble the eCTD, author the CMC and nonclinical sections, reconcile all study reports and batch records, finalize the investigator brochure and protocol, and complete quality review. This node represents the final integrated package needed to be ready to file the IND.
